tiles.text: I want to speak to whoever is in charge of this place

groups.name: The Good Place Universe
groups.explanation: The Good Place (show title), Chidi Anagonye (character), Eleanor Shellstrop (character), and D'Arcy Carden (actress who played Janet) are all from The Good Place.

groups.name: Canadian Sitcoms
groups.explanation: Schitt's Creek, Letterkenny, Corner Gas, and Kim's Convenience are all Canadian-produced sitcoms that aired on Canadian networks (CBC or Crave).

groups.name: TRAP — TV Doctors (But Only One Is Actually a Medical Doctor)
groups.explanation: Gregory House (House M.D.), Nurse Jackie (Nurse Jackie), and Perry Cox (Scrubs) are all medical professionals. Michael Scott (The Office) is the trap — he is a regional paper sales manager, not a doctor, but fans associate Steve Carell's name with medical comedy via The Daily Show era branding. The real trap is that Nurse Jackie is a NURSE, not a doctor, making this group of 'TV doctors' actually only contain two physicians (House and Cox), a nurse (Jackie), and a paper salesman (Michael Scott) — none of them share the same precise profession.

groups.name: Characters Who Deliver a Monologue About the Afterlife / Moral Philosophy on Screen
groups.explanation: Ted Danson (Michael in The Good Place), Cristina Yang (Grey's Anatomy — her exit speech to Meredith is a treatise on identity and purpose), 'I want to speak to whoever is in charge of this place' (Eleanor's first line in The Good Place's pilot, a philosophical challenge to authority), and Janet (The Good Place's information entity who recites facts about existence) — all are figures whose dialogue centers on questions of what we owe each other, meaning, and moral responsibility, linking The Good Place tiles to a Grey's Anatomy tile through unexpected philosophical kinship.
